brief_document.title: Stage contracts, brief document types, and generated codecs
brief_document.purpose: Lands the v2 stage contracts inside the stacked-dev package and makes the brief document itself a typed value the workflow family can carry. The three stage-contract schemas (scout-report, dev-report, review-report) are copied from the design-system canon, drift-gated byte-for-byte, and fed to aion codegen so the stage payload codecs are generated from the same schemas authors validate against (ADR-007, S5). The brief document and the pre-resolved reference context get hand-written Gleam types and codecs, round-tripped against this cluster's own seeded brief files, so every later brief (prompts, pipeline, enrichment, dispatch) builds on one typed vocabulary instead of unstructured document strings.
brief_document.task: Three strands of work in the examples/stacked-dev package. (1) Schemas: copy the three stage-contract canon files from docs/design-system/schemas/ into examples/stacked-dev/schemas/ under the package naming convention (canon uses hyphens and a .schema.json suffix: scout-report.schema.json becomes scout_report.json, dev-report.schema.json becomes dev_report.json, review-report.schema.json becomes review_report.json — codegen requires snake_case stems). Content is byte-identical; only the filename changes. A drift-gate script encodes exactly that rename mapping and runs from the gleeunit suite. (2) Codegen: aion codegen reads EVERY schemas/*.json in the project (verified against docs/guides/codegen.md and crates/aion-cli/src/codegen.rs) and fails the whole run on $ref/$defs. Of the six existing workflow-IO schemas, exactly one — schemas/onatopp_input.json — uses $ref/$defs; the other five are already inside the codegen v1 subset. So this brief inlines onatopp_input.json's $defs/workspace into its workspace property (a semantics-preserving rewrite; the file itself is retired later by BD-003, but C3 requires codegen --check green at THIS brief's landing, so the inline happens here — called out as a scope-forward edit). The generated module src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam (module name comes from gleam.toml name = aion_stacked_dev) will therefore also contain types for the six workflow-IO schemas; those are an unused byproduct kept in sync by --check — the workflow modules keep using the hand-written codecs in codecs_workflows.gleam, because workflow ERROR unions can never be generated (codegen v1 rejects unions; design non-goal). (3) Types and codecs: brief document types (authored fields, optional scout/dev/review blocks per requirement, optional execution block — mirroring docs/design-system/schemas/brief.schema.json field-for-field) and resolved-context types in src/stacked_dev/types.gleam, with hand-written codecs in a new module src/stacked_dev/codecs_brief.gleam (hand-written because the brief document is not a package dispatch schema; its canon lives in the design system — C4). Round-trip tests use docs/design/brief-dev/briefs/BD-001.json — this very file — as the seeded fixture (the checklist's 'seeded RM-011 brief files' phrasing predates the seeding; the brief-dev cluster's own briefs are the real seeded files). 'Byte-faithfully' is delivered as lossless-and-deterministic: decode loses nothing, encode is canonical and stable (decode-encode-decode yields an equal value and a byte-identical re-encode); reproducing the pretty-printed on-disk formatting is NOT required — on-disk writing belongs to BD-004's enrichment. Scope-forward additions, called out per BRIEF.md: ResolvedAdr carries decided_by beyond the minimal {id,title,decision,quote} so projections can attribute quotes to their speaker (P6/C7), and ResolvedContext carries intention, constraints, and design_path because PROMPTING.md's 'What Every Stage Gets' requires design context and the design file reference in every stage prompt — BD-002 consumes these. Note on C2/C3 wording: the drift gate 'runs as part of the test gate' via a gleeunit test in this brief; registering codegen --check 'in CI' beyond the command passing locally rides the cluster gates item C38 (BD-007). Coverage split: C4 and C5 are delivered across R4 (types), R5 (codecs), and R6 (round-trip tests). The new files codecs_brief.gleam, test/schema_drift_test.gleam, test/brief_codecs_test.gleam, and test/support/fixtures.* are not in the design.json structure array (the structure pins no unit-test or codec-module paths for this work); per CLAUDE.md the brief is authoritative over the structure annotation.

requirements.title: Copy the stage-contract canon into the package schemas
requirements.spec: Structural. Create examples/stacked-dev/schemas/scout_report.json, dev_report.json, and review_report.json as byte-identical copies of docs/design-system/schemas/scout-report.schema.json, dev-report.schema.json, and review-report.schema.json respectively. Only the filename changes (hyphen to underscore, .schema.json suffix dropped); every byte of content is preserved. The copies SHALL NOT be reformatted, re-indented, or edited in any way.
acceptance: diff docs/design-system/schemas/scout-report.schema.json examples/stacked-dev/schemas/scout_report.json exits 0.
diff docs/design-system/schemas/dev-report.schema.json examples/stacked-dev/schemas/dev_report.json exits 0.
diff docs/design-system/schemas/review-report.schema.json examples/stacked-dev/schemas/review_report.json exits 0.
python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('examples/stacked-dev/schemas/scout_report.json'))['title'])" prints 'Scout Report'.

requirements.title: Schema drift gate script wired into the test suite
requirements.spec: WHEN examples/stacked-dev/scripts/check-schema-drift.sh is invoked, THE SYSTEM SHALL compare each package stage-contract schema byte-for-byte (cmp) against its canon file using the fixed rename mapping (scout_report.json vs scout-report.schema.json, dev_report.json vs dev-report.schema.json, review_report.json vs review-report.schema.json), resolving both paths from the script's own location so the script works from any cwd. IF any pair differs or either file is missing, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL exit non-zero and print the name of every drifted or missing package schema. The script SHALL NOT rewrite either side, SHALL NOT compare the workflow-IO schemas (input.json, output.json, gate_input.json, gate_output.json — those are hand-owned, CN7 covers stage contracts only), and SHALL NOT exit zero while any pair differs. A gleeunit test (test/schema_drift_test.gleam) SHALL run the script through stacked_dev/cli.run from the package root and fail when the script exits non-zero, so the drift gate runs with the package test suite (C2).
acceptance: On a clean tree, bash examples/stacked-dev/scripts/check-schema-drift.sh exits 0 and its output names all three pairs checked.

After appending one newline byte to examples/stacked-dev/schemas/scout_report.json, the script exits 1 and its output contains 'scout_report.json'; restoring the byte-identical copy makes it exit 0 again.
After deleting examples/stacked-dev/schemas/dev_report.json, the script exits 1 and its output contains 'dev_report.json' (restore afterwards).
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam test runs schema_drift_test and it passes on a clean tree.

requirements.title: Make schemas/ codegen-clean and generate the IO module
requirements.spec: Structural, two steps. First: rewrite examples/stacked-dev/schemas/onatopp_input.json to inline its $defs/workspace definition directly into the workspace property and remove the $defs block — the inlined workspace object keeps the identical shape (required path/branch/placement/isolation, additionalProperties false, the same two enums), so dispatch-boundary validation behaviour is unchanged. This is required because aion codegen reads every schemas/*.json and fails loudly on $ref/$defs (codegen v1 subset), and onatopp_input.json is the only schema in the directory using them. Second: run cargo run -p aion-cli -- codegen examples/stacked-dev to generate src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam containing, per schema in filename order, a Gleam type plus <stem>_to_json and <stem>_decoder pairs — including ScoutReport, DevReport, and ReviewReport from the R1 copies. The generated module SHALL carry the do-not-edit header as its first line and SHALL NOT be edited by hand after generation; any schema change is followed by regeneration.
acceptance: grep -F '"$ref"' examples/stacked-dev/schemas/*.json produces no matches, and grep -F '"$defs"' examples/stacked-dev/schemas/*.json produces no matches.

python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('examples/stacked-dev/schemas/onatopp_input.json')); w=s['properties']['workspace']; assert w['required']==['path','branch','placement','isolation'] and w['additionalProperties'] is False" exits 0.
cargo run -p aion-cli -- codegen examples/stacked-dev --check exits 0 from the repo root.
head -1 examples/stacked-dev/src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam is exactly '//// Generated by aion codegen — do not edit; regenerate from schemas/.'

src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam declares pub type ScoutReport, pub type DevReport, and pub type ReviewReport with scout_report_to_json/scout_report_decoder, dev_report_to_json/dev_report_decoder, and review_report_to_json/review_report_decoder pairs.
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam build exits 0.

requirements.title: Brief document and resolved-context types
requirements.spec: Structural. Add to src/stacked_dev/types.gleam, mirroring docs/design-system/schemas/brief.schema.json field-for-field: BriefDocument(id, cluster, title, depends_on: List(String), blocked_by: List(String), checklist: List(String), stories: List(String), design_anchor: List(String), purpose, task, requirements: List(BriefRequirement), boundaries: List(String), verification: List(String), execution: option.Option(ExecutionBlock)); BriefRequirement(id, title, spec, acceptance: List(String), files: RequirementFiles, checklist: List(String), stories: List(String), scout: option.Option(ScoutBlock), dev: option.Option(DevBlock), review: option.Option(ReviewBlock)); RequirementFiles(create: List(String), modify: List(String), delete: List(String)); ScoutBlock(files: List(String), context: List(String), approach: String, notes: String); DevBlock(status: DevStatus, files_changed: List(FileChange), how: String, deviation: String, checklist: List(ChecklistClaim), stories: List(StoryClaim)) with DevStatus = Implemented | Blocked; FileChange(path: String, change: ChangeKind, note: String) with ChangeKind = Created | Modified | Deleted; ChecklistClaim(id: String, done: Bool, note: String); StoryClaim(id: String, satisfied: Bool, note: String); ReviewBlock(alignment: Alignment, acceptance: List(AcceptanceVerdict), checklist: List(String), stories: List(String), issues: List(String), fixes: List(String)) with Alignment = Aligned | Drifted | Fixed; AcceptanceVerdict(criterion: String, met: Bool, evidence: String); ExecutionBlock(status: ExecutionStatus, workflow_id: String, branch: String, session_id: String, gate: GateBlock, attestation: AttestationBlock, review_verdict: ExecutionVerdict, landed_commit: String, merged_into: String, completed_at: String) with ExecutionStatus = ExecutionInFlight | ExecutionLanded | ExecutionFailed (prefixed because the module already defines a Landed constructor) and ExecutionVerdict = VerdictApproved | VerdictChangesRequested | VerdictRejected (prefixed because ReviewDecision already defines Approve/Reject); GateBlock(fmt: Bool, clippy: Bool, tests: Bool, fix_rounds: Int); AttestationBlock(no_panics: Bool, no_unsafe: Bool, boundaries_respected: Bool, tests_pass: Bool). Resolved-context types: ResolvedAdr(id, title, decision, quote, decided_by — all String); ResolvedItem(id: String, text: String); ResolvedProvenance(requested_by: String, quote: String); ResolvedContext(adrs: List(ResolvedAdr), checklist: List(ResolvedItem), stories: List(ResolvedItem), constraints: List(ResolvedItem), intention: String, design_path: String, provenance: ResolvedProvenance). Only the enrichment blocks (scout/dev/review) and the execution block are Option; every authored field is non-optional — emptiness is authored, never defaulted (ADR-007 plus the brief schema's required list).
acceptance: BriefRequirement's scout, dev, and review fields and BriefDocument's execution field are option.Option types; no other BriefDocument or BriefRequirement field is an Option.
ExecutionStatus has exactly the constructors ExecutionInFlight, ExecutionLanded, ExecutionFailed, and ExecutionVerdict has exactly VerdictApproved, VerdictChangesRequested, VerdictRejected.

Every property name in brief.schema.json's root required list (id, cluster, title, depends_on, blocked_by, checklist, stories, design_anchor, purpose, task, requirements, boundaries, verification) has a same-named BriefDocument field.
ResolvedAdr has exactly the fields id, title, decision, quote, decided_by; ResolvedContext has exactly the fields adrs, checklist, stories, constraints, intention, design_path, provenance.
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam build exits 0 with the new types in place.

requirements.title: Hand-written codecs for the brief document and resolved context
requirements.spec: Create src/stacked_dev/codecs_brief.gleam exposing brief_document_codec() -> codec.Codec(BriefDocument) and resolved_context_codec() -> codec.Codec(ResolvedContext), plus brief_document_to_json/brief_document_decoder and resolved_context_to_json/resolved_context_decoder pairs following the codecs_core conventions (codec.json_codec over gleam/json encoders and gleam/dynamic/decode decoders). WHEN encoding, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit wire keys exactly matching the brief.schema.json property names and SHALL omit the scout/dev/review/execution keys entirely when the value is None; enum wire strings are exactly implemented/blocked, created/modified/deleted, aligned/drifted/fixed, in_flight/landed/failed, and approved/changes_requested/rejected. WHEN decoding, IF any required authored field is missing, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL fail with a codec.DecodeError naming the field path; THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT substitute a default for any missing field (ADR-001 discipline: emptiness is authored) and SHALL NOT accept enum strings outside the schema's sets. Resolved-context wire keys mirror the R4 field names (adrs, checklist, stories, constraints, intention, design_path, provenance with requested_by/quote). LOC note: the module is estimated near the 500-LOC cap; if the cap (excluding comments and whitespace) is breached during implementation, extract the enrichment-block encoders/decoders (scout/dev/review/execution) into src/stacked_dev/codecs_brief_blocks.gleam and declare the deviation in the dev report.
acceptance: brief_document_codec().decode("{\"id\":\"BD-001\"}") returns an Error whose reason names a missing required field (cluster is the first checked).

Encoding a BriefDocument whose requirements all have scout: None, dev: None, review: None and execution: None produces a JSON string containing no "scout" key, no "dev" key, no "review" key, and no "execution" key.
A DevBlock with status Implemented encodes status as the exact string "implemented" and decodes back to Implemented; the string "half-done" fails the DevStatus decode.
resolved_context_codec() round-trips a ResolvedContext holding ResolvedAdr("ADR-003", ..., decided_by: "Tom") to an equal value.
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam build exits 0.

requirements.title: Round-trip tests against the seeded brief fixture
requirements.spec: Create test/brief_codecs_test.gleam plus a file-reading test helper (test/support/fixtures.gleam wrapping a new test/support/fixtures_ffi.erl binding over Erlang file:read_file, returning Result(String, String)). WHEN the suite runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL read the seeded fixture at ../../docs/design/brief-dev/briefs/BD-001.json relative to the package root (gleam test runs from the package root), decode it with brief_document_codec(), and verify the lossless deterministic round-trip; the tests SHALL NOT use an invented brief shape as the primary fixture (the seeded authored file is the contract) and SHALL NOT write to the fixture.
acceptance: Decoding the fixture succeeds; the decoded value has id "BD-001", cluster "brief-dev", and exactly 6 requirements, every one with scout == None, dev == None, review == None, and the document's execution == None.
Re-decoding brief_document_codec().encode(decoded) yields a value equal to the first decode.
Encoding is deterministic: encode(decode(encode(decoded))) is byte-identical to encode(decoded).
Decoding the literal "{\"id\":\"BD-001\"}" fails with a DecodeError.

resolved_context_codec() round-trips a ResolvedContext whose adrs list contains ADR-003 with quote "we shouldn't have a default timeout, the agent steps can take well over an hour" and decided_by "Tom" to an equal value.
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam test passes.

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify any file under docs/design-system/schemas/ — the canon is upstream; the package copies adapt to it, never the reverse.
SHALL NOT create or modify enrich.gleam, any enrich_brief activity, or any brief-file writing — enrichment is BD-004.
SHALL NOT create prompts.gleam or any prompt projection — BD-002.

SHALL NOT modify stacked_dev.gleam, onatopp_dev.gleam, gate.gleam, activities.gleam, locals.gleam, codecs_workflows.gleam, or workflow.toml — the pipeline rebuild is BD-003/BD-005; this brief's only edit outside its new files is the $defs inline in schemas/onatopp_input.json and the type additions in types.gleam.
SHALL NOT touch examples/stacked-dev/worker/ — worker handlers and wire_compat pins are BD-005.
SHALL NOT create dispatch.gleam or any dispatch/wave schema — BD-006.

SHALL NOT modify test/aion_stacked_dev_test.gleam, test/support/shims.gleam, stacked_dev_test_ffi.erl, crates/aion-cli/templates/dev_pipeline/, or crates/aion-cli/tests/stacked_dev_live_e2e.rs — hermetic/e2e suites and the template are BD-007.
SHALL NOT hand-edit src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam — schema change then regenerate is the only path.
SHALL NOT delete or rename any of the six existing workflow-IO schemas; the onatopp pair's retirement is BD-003's.
verification: bash examples/stacked-dev/scripts/check-schema-drift.sh exits 0 on the landed tree.

diff docs/design-system/schemas/scout-report.schema.json examples/stacked-dev/schemas/scout_report.json; diff docs/design-system/schemas/dev-report.schema.json examples/stacked-dev/schemas/dev_report.json; diff docs/design-system/schemas/review-report.schema.json examples/stacked-dev/schemas/review_report.json — all three exit 0.
grep -F '"$ref"' examples/stacked-dev/schemas/*.json and grep -F '"$defs"' examples/stacked-dev/schemas/*.json both produce no matches.
cargo run -p aion-cli -- codegen examples/stacked-dev --check exits 0.
head -1 examples/stacked-dev/src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam prints the generated-module do-not-edit header.
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam build && gleam test both exit 0 (includes the drift-gate test and the BD-001.json round-trip suite).
python3 docs/design-system/scripts/validate.py docs/design/brief-dev/briefs/BD-001.json reports OK — the fixture the round-trip suite reads is still schema-valid.

The diff touches no file owned by BD-002 through BD-007: no prompts.gleam, no enrich.gleam, no dispatch.gleam, nothing under worker/, no change to stacked_dev.gleam, onatopp_dev.gleam, workflow.toml, test/aion_stacked_dev_test.gleam, or test/support/shims.gleam.
grep -n 'onatopp_input' examples/stacked-dev/schemas/onatopp_input.json confirms the file still exists (this brief inlines its $defs; it is deleted only in BD-003).

adrs.title: No backwards compatibility during the build
adrs.decision: Replace, don't add alongside. No compat shims, no zombie code, no #[deprecated] markers. Breaking changes are made cleanly and consumers move forward. Rejected: incremental deprecation cycles — they double the surface under test for an audience of zero.

adrs.title: Design system v2: JSON ledgers above clusters, enrichment in place
adrs.decision: Two project ledgers (roadmap.json, decisions.json) above the clusters; stage contracts as first-class schemas inside the aion codegen subset; the brief is one living document — the pipeline appends scout/dev/review per requirement and an execution block per brief, in place, never touching authored fields. Rejected: a sibling runs/ ledger — the brief as a single spec-plus-record document was the original intent, and aion's event history already provides the append-only audit trail.
adrs.quote: basically stuff is just depended to it so I would actually be happy to have it to have it saved back in place from where it came from

checklist.text: schemas/scout_report.json, dev_report.json, and review_report.json exist in the package and are byte-identical to their docs/design-system/schemas/ canon files.
scripts/check-schema-drift.sh fails loudly when any package stage-contract schema diverges from canon, and runs as part of the test gate.
src/aion_stacked_dev_io.gleam is generated by aion codegen from the package schemas, carries the do-not-edit header, and aion codegen --check passes clean in CI.

Brief document types (authored fields + optional scout/dev/review per requirement + optional execution block) are defined in Gleam with hand-written codecs that round-trip the cluster's own brief files (fixture: docs/design/brief-dev/briefs/BD-001.json) losslessly with deterministic encoding.
Resolved-context types (ADR texts for design_anchor, C#/S# texts, roadmap provenance quote) are defined with codecs; workflow code never sees an unresolved reference.

stories.text: As Claude, I want stage payloads validated against the same schemas I author briefs against so that a malformed document breaks at dispatch with a pointer, not mid-run in an agent prompt.

constraints.text: Deterministic workflow code performs no filesystem reads, no ledger parsing, and no reference resolution: dispatcher activities resolve roadmap/decisions/checklist/stories references and assemble the brief context before any workflow logic sees it (P5).

Every cap, backoff, deadline, and concurrency limit is a required input field, schema-enforced with additionalProperties: false; the engine and the workflows impose no bound of their own (ADR-001, ADR-003).
enrich_brief is append-only: it refuses to alter any authored field (deep-compares the authored subset before writing) and fails terminally on divergence rather than silently overwriting.

Stage sessions are deterministic and disjoint: '<branch>' for dev (existing), '<branch>-scout' for scout, '<branch>-review' for review. The reviewer NEVER shares the dev session — fresh eyes are the point of the stage (REVIEW.md).
Activity failure taxonomy: can't-execute is terminal failure; expected negative outcomes are successful results carrying data (P3). No retries of broken environments.
The family deploys as a unit (shared content hash, child type = entry module name); all archives rebuilt and redeployed together on any change.
Package stage-contract schemas are byte-identical to docs/design-system/schemas/ canon, enforced by a drift gate that runs with the test suite.

The outer arc's live-proven CLI contracts are immutable in this cluster: request_review argv (branch positional first, --as Meridian), land = git commit in worktree then yg merge from repo_root, single review_verdict signal (ADR-006).
Hermetic tests run under gleeunit's sequential runner (exclusive PATH is VM-global); e2e suites never run concurrently with gleam builds in the same checkout.

resolved_context.intention: Design-system v2 briefs become executable: an all-norn pipeline (scout → dev → verify-fix → adversarial review → harden) runs as a durable aion workflow, enriching the brief in place at every stage, composed under the live-proven stacked_dev outer arc (provision → dev → gate → human review → land), with a dispatcher above that selects briefed work from the roadmap ledger and fans it out. When this is done, 'do this roadmap item' means authoring documents and approving a DM — the workflow does the rest, every stage durable across crashes, every claim recorded next to what was actually measured. aion develops aion; the dogfood becomes the dev process.

provenance.quote: Design-system v2 briefs become executable: an all-norn pipeline (scout → dev → verify-fix → adversarial review → harden) runs as a durable aion workflow, enriching the brief in place at every stage, c
